authoritative_sources.title: Assurance of Transportation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Medicaid.gov publishes federal Medicaid transportation guidance. It identifies state plan assurance of necessary transportation, NEMT policy, EPSDT transportation assistance, and a Medicaid Transportation Coverage Guide, making it directly authoritative for healthcare travel operations prerequisites and covered-transport boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: transport benefit prerequisite, covered travel readiness, and transport exception routing
used_for: NEMT and medical travel prerequisite handling
transport execution versus coverage or medical-necessity boundary
scenario design for missing documentation and conflicting broker rules

authoritative_sources.title: Non-Emergency Medical Transportation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS provider and beneficiary resources describe NEMT benefit operations, emergency versus non-emergency boundaries, delivery systems, accepted transport types, driver and vehicle criteria, and fraud or abuse considerations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: transport booking, broker handoff, mode verification, and fraud or abuse-sensitive exception handling
used_for: transport denial and no-show failure modes

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Eligibility Transaction System (HETS) 270/271
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS operates the Medicare eligibility transaction system. HETS anchors eligibility evidence, 270/271 terminology, and the distinction between active coverage evidence and final travel or service readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility verification and coverage evidence capture

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule (CMS-0057-F)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS-0057-F is federal public policy for impacted payer prior authorization processes, status transparency, and denial-reason communication. It supports authorization status workflows without authorizing this lane to approve or interpret payer decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization status handling and payer dependency escalation
used_for: conflicting public baseline and tenant policy scenario

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Managed Care Appeals & Grievances
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS managed care guidance is authoritative for Medicare health plan grievance, organization determination, coverage determination, reconsideration, and appeal pathways. It supports escalation boundaries when travel-benefit complaints become formal payer or appeal work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: complaint, organization determination, reconsideration, denial, and appeal owner routing

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. Minimum-necessary guidance directly constrains PHI collection, vendor routing, traveler communications, escalation packets, and completion evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy-gated intake, evidence packaging, vendor communication, and stakeholder communication

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance explains permitted use and disclosure of PHI for treatment, payment, and health care operations while requiring role-based access and other limits. It constrains healthcare travel operations communications with members, families, care teams, vendors, and payers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient/member communication, proxy/requester handling, operations routing, and vendor handoff

authoritative_sources.title: CAQH CORE Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE is designated by HHS as the national operating rule authoring entity for HIPAA-covered administrative transactions. Its rules ground eligibility, claim status, remittance, prior authorization, referrals, attachments, acknowledgements, and error handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, claim status, remittance, prior authorization, referral, attachment, acknowledgement, and error workflow

authoritative_sources.title: CAHPS Health Plan Survey
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ CAHPS Health Plan Survey is a national standard for measuring consumer experience with health plans, including getting needed care, getting care quickly, and health plan customer service. It anchors customer-impact and repeat-failure framing for travel operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient/member service experience, access-to-care impact, and complaint classification
used_for: appointment-miss and pickup failure failure modes

authoritative_sources.title: National Performance Goal #1: Right Patient, Right Care
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Joint Commission is a recognized healthcare accrediting body. Its right-patient/right-care focus supports identity and service-matching escalation when travel operations could route, dispatch, or communicate about the wrong patient/member.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient identity, readiness, handoff, and patient-safety escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Passengers with Disabilities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public travel accessibility or passenger-rights guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOT enforces air-travel disability protections and official passenger guidance. It is authoritative for accessible assistance, seating, service animal, and disability-sensitive air-travel escalation boundaries in medical-traveler operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accessible air-travel support, disability-sensitive assistance, and travel accessibility escalation

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Minimum prerequisites include patient/member identity, requester authority, consent or proxy status, approved communication channel, facility, service date, appointment or encounter context, payer and plan, eligibility, benefits, referral requirements, prior authorization status, transport benefit or broker authorization, lodging policy, discharge or care-transition readiness, required documentation for transport mode, and tenant source hierarchy.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include EHR or EMR, master patient index, registration and scheduling, practice management, real-time eligibility systems, payer portals, referral and authorization systems, utilization review, case management, discharge planning, CRM, contact-center and communications systems, transportation broker or dispatch platforms, lodging and booking systems, privacy and consent systems, revenue-cycle systems, and claim/remittance/denial/appeal systems. Tenant source hierarchy resolves conflicts.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, symptom triage, discharge instruction changes, clinical travel clearance, medical necessity, utilization review judgment, authorization approval, payer policy interpretation, appeal strategy, coding assignment, claim correction, privacy-release approval, legal interpretation, financial approval, refund or compensation approval, and safety-critical transport clearance.

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and lifecycle classification
workflow_stages.description: Normalize patient/member, guest, medical traveler, care-team, broker, lodging, or transport request intent; establish current lifecycle state; verify requester authority, identity, channel, service date, facility, payer, plan, and requested action.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and system-of-record retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, appointment, encounter, discharge, care-transition, case-management, utilization-review, PHI, consent, travel, broker, dispatch, lodging, claim, denial, appeal, and communication records from authoritative systems.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Travel booking, transport, lodging, and readiness processing
workflow_stages.description: Move administrative work through ready-to-book, booked, confirmed, and in-transit states when required evidence is present; document pickup windows, trip legs, lodging confirmations, communication status, open caveats, and customer-impact notes.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Public travel accessibility or passenger-rights guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, hold, rework, and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Place cases in exception, hold, or rework when prerequisites fail, records conflict, broker or supplier status changes, PHI handling is unresolved, or clinical, payer, privacy, financial, legal, or safety authority is required.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Close only when fulfillment, traveler communication, exception resolution, evidence references, unresolved items, and closure rationale are recorded; refuse completion when itinerary creation is not proof of travel fulfillment.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: Patient/member identifiers, MRN, member ID, requester identity, relationship, proxy or consent records, communication preferences, approved channel, and privacy restrictions
Eligibility and benefits records, 270/271 responses, payer/plan identifiers, referral records, prior authorization status, authorization numbers, payer portal status, and attachment acknowledgements
Appointment, encounter, order, clinical-administrative note, discharge summary, care-transition record, case-management note, and utilization-review record

Travel request, traveler profile, itinerary, origin, destination, trip legs, pickup and drop-off windows, transport mode, escort or attendant need, accessibility equipment, broker trip ID, dispatch log, no-show or cancellation log, lodging confirmation, accessible-room note, and vendor communication record
Charge capture, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, reimbursement, service recovery, payment guarantee, and revenue-cycle routing artifacts when relevant
Execution status updates, travel readiness summaries, exception queue notes, rework or hold summaries, escalation notes, traveler communication summaries, and completion evidence records
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
Master patient index, registration, scheduling, and patient access systems
Practice management, revenue cycle, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, and reimbursement systems
Real-time eligibility, benefits verification, payer portal, referral, authorization, and utilization review systems
Case management, care transition, discharge planning, CRM, contact-center, complaint, and patient/member communication systems
Transportation broker, NEMT platform, dispatch, ride manifest, supplier portal, booking, lodging, travel management, and itinerary systems
Consent, proxy, privacy restriction, role-based access, audit, and identity verification systems

allowed: Classify travel operations work as accepted, prerequisite-check, ready-to-book, booked, confirmed, in-transit, exception, rework, held, escalated, completed, or closed-with-evidence.
Request minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Summarize documented eligibility, referral, authorization, appointment, discharge, transport, lodging, claim, denial, or appeal status without interpreting clinical or payer meaning.
Move administrative travel execution forward when records, tenant policy, and delegated authority support the state transition.
Prepare execution status updates, readiness summaries, exception queue notes, rework summaries, traveler communication summaries, routing notes, and completion evidence records.
escalate: Required patient/member, payer, plan, facility, service date, referral, authorization, encounter, discharge, transport, lodging, privacy, broker, supplier, or tenant policy facts are missing or conflict.

The request involves PHI disclosure uncertainty, proxy or consent ambiguity, identity mismatch, patient-safety risk, clinical judgment, medical necessity, payer policy interpretation, authorization approval, coding, claim correction, denial appeal, refund, waiver, reimbursement, compensation, legal authority, or safety-critical transport clearance.
A no-show, missed pickup, accessible transport failure, lodging failure, formal complaint, repeat service failure, transport benefit denial, or service recovery request exceeds documented authority.
Public baseline guidance and tenant policy, payer contract, state Medicaid rule, broker instruction, facility rule, supplier policy, or jurisdictional requirement materially conflict.
refuse: Disclose PHI when requester authority, approved channel, consent, proxy status, role-based access, or minimum-necessary purpose is unresolved.
Diagnose, triage, change discharge instructions, recommend treatment, approve medical necessity, or imply licensed clinical judgment or clinical travel clearance.

Approve or deny authorization, interpret payer policy as binding, override payer or broker prerequisites, assign codes, correct claims, draft appeals, approve refunds, grant reimbursement, or promise compensation outside delegated authority.
Bypass patient identity, eligibility, referral, authorization, documentation, privacy, patient-safety, transport, lodging, broker, service recovery, or completion evidence controls.

domain_failure_modes: the lane treats active eligibility as travel readiness even though referral, authorization, transport benefit, broker, lodging, or discharge dependencies remain missing
PHI is disclosed to a family member, companion, employer, hotel, airline, transportation broker, or supplier without verified requester authority and minimum-necessary controls
a travel case is closed after itinerary creation even though transport completion, lodging confirmation, communication, and exception evidence are missing
transport mode or clinical travel readiness is inferred from symptoms, diagnosis, or discharge timing rather than routed to licensed clinical or utilization review ownership
payer or broker denial reasons are reinterpreted by the travel lane instead of routed to payer operations, utilization management, or appeals ownership
conflicting EHR, payer portal, broker, CRM, dispatch, lodging, or tenant policy records are synthesized without source hierarchy or human resolution
customer-impact pressure causes identity matching, authorization, referral, privacy, or patient-safety controls to be bypassed
travel operations work collapses into generic concierge or travel-agent behavior and omits patient/member, payer, authorization, PHI, and care-transition evidence

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State Medicaid NEMT rules, broker manuals, and payer contracts vary materially.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The package requires tenant, jurisdiction, payer/product, state Medicaid, broker, and facility context before travel execution. Conflicts route to patient access, utilization management, payer operations, or orchestrator return.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Medical travel programs may combine hospitality benefits, charity programs, covered NEMT, and discretionary lodging.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The lane can execute only documented administrative steps and must escalate benefit, reimbursement, waiver, service recovery, and financial approval decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Clinical travel readiness and discharge timing can change quickly.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The lane requires licensed clinical or care-team documentation for clinical clearance and must hold or route cases when clinical status controls travel execution.

blocking_ambiguity_summary.notes: No blocking ambiguity remains for a specification-based validated package because variable state, payer, facility, broker, supplier, privacy, and clinical rules are explicit retrieval dependencies and escalation conditions.
